figures.caption: Figure 1: An example from LIMIT-H demonstrating ambiguity of the generated document identifiers (italic) that prevent the GR model from distinguishing between relevant (green) and irrelevant (red) documents.
Figure 2: Comparison of Recall@2 performance across three increasing levels of difficulty.

Table 3: Mean and standard deviation of R@2 for three variants (“likes”, “owns”, “touches”) each of LIMIT, LIMIT-H and LIMIT-HS. Bold indicates the best overall performance (excluding the oracle configuration). † indicates statistically significant improvement (𝑝 < 0.05) over the strong BM25 baseline (paired t-test).
Table 4: Error analysis of SEAL in the default configuration. The model fails to retrieve any relevant documents at the top-2 ranks for LIMIT-H and LIMIT-HS, resulting in no score being computed (-).
Table 5: Error analysis of SEAL in the classical beam search configuration (BEAM).

Table 7: Sample documents with oracle pseudo-queries from LIMIT-H for the original query “Who likes Hot Chocolate?”. Given this query, MINDER generates only the ngram: “Hot Chocolate”, and no pseudo-queries scored above zero.

Table 6: Error analysis ofMINDER under the (BEAM, NQ+PQ) configuration, which employs manually constructed pseudoqueries for the original documents and few-shot LLM generated queries for negative examples.
Table 2: Retrieval results on the original LIMIT dataset (50k documents). Baseline results (*) are from Weller et al. [18]. Bold indicates the best overall performance.
